step_by_step_thinking:
Asherman's syndrome typically results in abnormal scarring and adhesion formation within the uterus, leading to changes in menstrual flow. Commonly observed features include oligomenorrhea (infrequent periods) and metrorrhagia (irregular spotting between periods). Given the description of Asherman's syndrome focusing primarily on altered menstrual patterns:
\boxed{A} Based on the detailed descriptions of Asherman's syndrome, including its effects on menstrual regularity, oligomenorrhea stands out as a characteristic feature due to reduced frequency of menses resulting from scarred and adherent uterine walls. Therefore,
